blocks.text: had confirmed the window. 9:43 to 10:30 — that was real time with real edges.
had not committed to the hour yet. He needed the evidence to hold it first.
held it without committing to it out loud.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Officially â€” we are treating this as a suspicious death pending toxicology.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: What does that mean for us?

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: It means none of you leave the county until I say otherwise. We'll need formal statements from each of you. If tox comes back clean it may well be nothing.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: And if it doesn't come back clean?

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Then we deal with that when we get there.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: You think one of us â€”

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Ms. Laurent. I am asking everyone in this house to cooperate and stay available. That is all I am doing right now.
Nadia's expression didn't change. The others showed fear or grief or both. She had already settled into something past that.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: Whatever you need, Inspector. Marcus deserves the truth.
She meant it. I was almost certain she meant it.

blocks.prompt: Nadia said Marcus deserves the truth. Does she mean it?

options.label: Yes. Even now, the grief is real.
She said exactly what someone would say. That's not the same as meaning it.
